start.speaker: System
start.text: Welcome to ROI - Return on Investment. Choose your quest from the map to begin your financial journey.

lvl1_scene1.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl1_scene1.text: I just received my ₹10,000 stipend! Now I have to decide how to manage it.

lvl2_scene1.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl2_scene1.text: I just received my first salary — ₹50,000! I feel like I'm on top of the world. Time to celebrate!

choices.text: Go out with friends to celebrate

lvl2_father_call.speaker: Father
lvl2_father_call.text: Congratulations on your first salary, beta! I'm proud. But remember, managing a salary is different. Try the 50-30-20 rule: 50% for Needs, 30% for Wants, and 20% for Savings.

choices.text: How does the 50-30-20 rule work exactly?

lvl2_503020_explain.speaker: Father
lvl2_503020_explain.text: It's simple: Spend ₹25,000 on Needs (rent, bills), ₹15,000 on Wants (movies, hobbies), and save ₹10,000 for your future and emergencies.

lvl2_choice_month.speaker: System
lvl2_choice_month.text: Month 1 Salary (₹50,000): How will you allocate your budget?

choices.mentorText: Perfect! This balances your present joy with future security.
Living large! But what happens if an emergency strikes?

lvl2_emi_trap.speaker: Friend
lvl2_emi_trap.text: Look at this new flagship phone! It's ₹60,000, but you can get it for just ₹5000 EMI per month. It's basically free!

choices.mentorText: EMIs are attractive but they eat into your future monthly budget.

choices.text: No, I'll save up and buy it later.
choices.mentorText: Wise choice. Avoid the debt trap early in your career.

lvl2_emi_warning.speaker: Father
lvl2_emi_warning.text: Careful, beta. Multiple small EMIs can slowly drain your salary until you have nothing left for savings.

lvl2_needs_quiz.speaker: System
lvl2_needs_quiz.text: Time for a quick quiz! Classify these items as NEED or WANT.

lvl2_quiz_q2.speaker: System
lvl2_quiz_q2.text: Classify: Designer shoes, Gaming console, Netflix subscription

lvl2_emergency_fund.speaker: Father
lvl2_emergency_fund.text: One more thing: Always build an Emergency Fund. Life is unpredictable, and having 3-6 months of expenses saved can save you from high-interest loans later.

choices.text: I'll start building my emergency fund today!

lvl2_6month_skip.speaker: System
lvl2_6month_skip.text: 6 Months Later... Your car broke down suddenly! The repair bill is ₹20,000. Do you have enough in your emergency fund?

choices.text: Check if my savings cover it

lvl2_emergency_outcome.speaker: System
lvl2_emergency_outcome.text: Assessing your savings and EMI commitments...

lvl2_final_summary.speaker: System
lvl2_final_summary.text: Level 2 complete! You've mastered the 50-30-20 rule, avoided the EMI trap, and understood the value of an emergency fund.

lvl3_scene1.speaker: Friend
lvl3_scene1.text: Hey! Let's split the bill for that dinner. I'll pay, you send me your part.

choices.text: Wait, I'll just use my card.

lvl3_card_confusion.speaker: Friend
lvl3_card_confusion.text: Use credit, it's easier. You don't have to pay right now.

choices.text: Debit or credit... what's the real difference?

lvl3_card_explain.speaker: Friend
lvl3_card_explain.text: Debit uses your own money from your account instantly. Credit lets you spend now and pay the bank later. It's like a short-term loan.

choices.mentorText: Good. Using your own money keeps you out of depth.

choices.text: Use Credit Card (Spend now, pay later)
choices.mentorText: Pay attention! This money isn't yours. You'll have to pay it back at the end of the month.

lvl3_bank_talk.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl3_bank_talk.text: Most of my money just sits in my savings account... Does it even do anything?

lvl3_brother_advice.speaker: Elder Brother
lvl3_brother_advice.text: That's your savings account. It gives you interest on your money. It grows, but very slowly. It's safe, but not very exciting if you want to build wealth.

choices.text: Is there a way to make it grow faster?

lvl3_invest_intro.speaker: Friend
lvl3_invest_intro.text: Bro, I started investing in mutual funds and stocks and made some good profit! You should try it too.

lvl3_invest_options.speaker: Father
lvl3_invest_options.text: Start simple. FD (Fixed Deposit) is safe with fixed returns. Mutual Funds are managed by professionals. Stocks are high risk but can give huge returns.

lvl3_invest_choice.speaker: System
lvl3_invest_choice.text: You have ₹500,000 in savings. How would you like to invest it?

choices.mentorText: Reliable and safe. A great foundation.
Diversified! A professional approach to growth.
Going for the gold! Be prepared for market volatility.

lvl3_time_lapse.speaker: System
lvl3_time_lapse.text: 3 Months Later... Let's see how your investments performed.

lvl3_outcome.speaker: System
lvl3_outcome.text: Assessing market performance and calculating your wealth growth...

lvl3_realisation.speaker: Adam/Eve
lvl3_realisation.text: I get it now. Saving keeps money safe, but investing helps it grow. And credit needs to be controlled wisely!
